questions.question: A rectangular prism has length 5 cm, width 4 cm, and height 3 cm. What is its volume?
questions.explanation: Volume of a rectangular prism = length × width × height = 5 × 4 × 3 = 60 cm³.

questions.question: A cylinder has a radius of 2 inches and a height of 7 inches. Using π ≈ 3.14, what is its volume to the nearest whole number?
questions.explanation: Volume of a cylinder = πr²h = 3.14 × 2² × 7 = 3.14 × 4 × 7 = 87.92 ≈ 88 cubic inches.

questions.question: A sphere has a radius of 3 cm. Using π ≈ 3.14, what is its volume to the nearest whole number?
A cone has a base radius of 4 meters and a height of 6 meters. Using π ≈ 3.14, what is its volume to the nearest whole number?
A triangular prism has a triangular base with area 12 square inches and a height (length) of 5 inches. What is the volume?
questions.explanation: Volume of any prism = base area × height = 12 × 5 = 60 cubic inches. The base is the triangular face.

questions.question: If a cube has a side length of 4 cm, what is its volume?
questions.explanation: A cube is a special rectangular prism where all sides are equal. Volume = side³ = 4³ = 64 cm³.
